MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES PLAINTIFFS MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND/OR ALTERNATIVELY SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES (TRANSACTION ID # 100115799) FILED BY PLAINTIFF PRASAD, RANJUNA
ORDER GRANTING RYDER TRUCK RENTAL LT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RYDER TRUCK RENTAL LT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION: DEFENDANT RYDER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. RYDER ESTABLISHES THAT IT SOLD THE VEHICLE PRIOR TO THE ACCIDENT, COMPLIED WITH VEHICLE CODE SEC. 5602 WHEN IT TRANSFERRED TITLE, MR. FURTADO WAS NOT RYDER'S AGENT, AND RYDER CANNOT BE LIABLE UNDER VEHICLE CODE SEC. 17150. THE COURT OVERRULES PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATION OF JOSE AMEZCUA. (SEE JAZAYERI V. MAO (2009) 174 CAL.APP.4TH 301, 322 [ANY QUALIFIED WITNESS MAY ESTABLISH THE FOUNDATION FOR BUSINESS RECORDS].) IN OPPOSITION, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRESENT ANY EVIDENCE TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: ROSE MILLER, CSR#5041, EMAIL: RMILLERCSR@GMAIL.COM. =(302/EPS)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Ryder Truck Rental Lt Motion Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-19-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Bradley Kass, Esq. (650-579-0612; kassoffice@sbcglobal.net) of Kass & Kass Law Offices for Plaintiff.
Nicholas Shepard, Esq. (916-978-3434; nshepard@mathenysears.com) of Matheny Sears...LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT RYDER TRUCK RENTAL LT's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication:

Defendant Ryder's motion for summary judgment is granted. Ryder establishes that it sold the vehicle prior to the accident, complied with Vehicle Code sec. 5602 when it transferred title, Mr. Furtado was not Ryder's agent, and Ryder cannot be liable under Vehicle Code sec. 17150. The Court overrules plaintiff's objections to the declaration of Jose Amezcua. (See Jazayeri v. Mao (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 301, 322 [any qualified witness may establish the foundation for business records].) In opposition, plaintiff fails to present any evidence to create a triable issue of material fact.

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041, email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. =(302/EPS)
